title: Feuilleea viscidula
taxonomy: Feuilleea viscidula is classified within the family Celastraceae, which includes other flowering plants commonly known as the bittersweet or staff vine family. The genus Feuilleea is part of the larger group of deciduous shrubs and vines. This plant is native to South Africa, where it grows in a variety of habitats. The Celastraceae family is characterized by its small, often fragrant flowers and unique fruit. As part of the Celastraceae family, Feuilleea viscidula shares similarities with other plants in its classification.

intro: Feuilleea viscidula, also known as Lomatium, is a perennial wildflower that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Feuilleea viscidula include sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
